title: SQL: UPDATE table SET column = null fails
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(50), value DECIMAL(10, 2), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, value) VALUES (1, 'Test', 10.50);
UPDATE test_table SET value = NULL WHERE id = 1;
CREATE TABLE another_table (ref_id INT, description TEXT, FOREIGN KEY (ref_id) REFERENCES test_table(id));
INSERT INTO another_table (ref_id, description) VALUES (1, 'Reference to Test');
key_bug_features: UPDATE statement setting column to null
